qualifying_org_definition: Any program or project serving a useful public purpose; explicitly includes health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban or rural development, welfare, recreation, public safety, or daycare — and even work for friends or relatives. No 501(c)(3) or nonprofit registration requirement stated in the policy; the activity must be beneficial, verifiable, and unpaid.
hours_formula_detail: Chapter 17 of the DHR Points of Eligibility manual explicitly states ABAWDs must meet '20 hour/week work requirement (80 hours a month)' — follows the federal baseline exactly.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Alabama policy (Chapter 7, Section 710A) defines qualifying volunteer work by activity type and public purpose, with no requirement that the organization be in-state, registered, or hold 501(c)(3) status. The policy is silent on org location. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) does not restrict org location; absent state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Alabama has no active ABAWD waivers in 2026. The FY2026 State Plan confirmed anticipated ABAWDs in waived areas = 0. Post-OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025), all prior waivers expired or were terminated by November 2025; Alabama had none previously either.
submission_deadline: Changes in work hours must be reported by the 10th of the month after the month in which the change occurred (Chapter 17, Section 1700). The six-month report form (for income/household changes) must be returned between the 1st and 10th of the sixth month of the certification period. ABAWDs have a 4-month certification period.
notes:
Alabama explicitly allows unpaid community service at nonprofits (health, social service, day care) and churches to satisfy the ABAWD 80-hour/month requirement. Alabama Legal Help guidance (sourced from DHR policy) describes no mandatory intermediate steps such as EDP, county assignment, MOU, or provider registration before volunteer hours count. A-RESET (Alabama's E&T program) is voluntary for SNAP participants, not a mandatory ABAWD prestep. No named state form for volunteer/community service verification was found; documentation appears to be a signed letter or timesheet from the org, reviewed by the DHR worker. Confidence is medium rather than high because Chapter 7 of the DHR policy manual (the definitive source) was only accessible as an unreadable binary PDF.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted a targeted adversarial search across six angles designed to refute the Tier 1 classification: (1) mandatory work registration / intake enrollment before volunteer hours count; (2) EDP or case manager assignment as a prerequisite; (3) approved volunteer site list or nonprofit MOU/registration requirement; (4) A-RESET as a mandatory gateway for ABAWDs; (5) county-level variation; (6) legal aid and anti-hunger org explainers that might surface hidden gating. Queries included: Alabama SNAP ABAWD work registration intake 2025-2026; Alabama SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer community service track; Alabama SNAP approved volunteer site list nonprofit agreement DHR; Alabama SNAP ABAWD case manager employment development plan 2025-2026. Fetched: Alabama Legal Help ABAWD brochure PDF (binary, unreadable), Appendix H forms list (binary, unreadable), FY2026 State Plan PDF (binary, unreadable), Chapter 7 POE manual (binary, unreadable), Alabama Legal Help SNAP ABAWD resource page (readable; confirms community service counts with no prestep described), WIOA state plan node (connection refused), DHR E&T procurement page (voluntary language; no mandatory ABAWD gateway), Ballotpedia (no Alabama-specific prestep found). Zero evidence was found of a mandatory EDP, state-imposed case manager assignment, pre-approved site list, or MOU/registration requirement for nonprofits. Every readable source that addresses A-RESET confirms it is voluntary. The FY2026 E&T State Plan search snippet confirmed 'A-RESET... voluntary.' The adversarial pass found nothing to refute Tier 1. Tier 1 classification is retained. Confidence remains 'medium' (not upgraded to 'high') because the primary policy authority — Chapter 7 of the DHR Points of Eligibility manual — is only available as a binary-unreadable PDF and cannot be directly quoted. The verification_instrument field also remains 'unknown' because no public-facing verification form or letter template number was surfaced in any readable source (an internal form DHR-FAD-657 ABAWD/E&T Addendum was mentioned in a search snippet but its content is not publicly confirmed as the org-facing verification mechanism). Remote stance remains unknown; no public source addressed it.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully extracted full text from two previously binary-unreadable PDFs: (1) DHR Points of Eligibility Chapter 7 (apps.dhr.alabama.gov/POE/chapters/Chapter%207.pdf, 17 pages, downloaded 326 KB) and (2) FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan (dhr.alabama.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/StatePlan_AL_2026_Approved.pdf, 178 pages, downloaded 1.2 MB). Both were successfully read via pypdf. Chapter 7, Section 710A is now directly quoted. It confirms that qualifying ABAWD work activities include: '(3) Performing beneficial and verifiable unpaid volunteer work for a program or project which serves a useful public purpose; such volunteer work may include but is not limited to providing improvements to public facilities or performing work in fields such as health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban or rural development, welfare, recreation, public safety or daycare including work for friends or relatives.' This is a broad qualifying org definition with NO restriction to 501(c)(3)s, approved site lists, or formal org registration. The manual imposes no EDP, case manager assignment, or pre-clearance before volunteer hours count — the activity simply must be 'beneficial and verifiable.' The word 'verifiable' appears in the policy but no specific verification form is named; Section 702 names DHR-WR-1802A for work registration only, not for volunteer hour documentation. The FY2026 State Plan confirms state-level administration, voluntary A-RESET (not a mandatory ABAWD gateway), and no county-level waivers (anticipated ABAWDs in waived areas = 0). Confidence is upgraded to HIGH: the primary policy source (Chapter 7 manual) was directly read and confirms the Tier 1 lane shape, supported by the FY2026 State Plan and multiple secondary sources — all consistent, none contradicting. The qualifying_org_definition has been updated to reflect the broader language actually in the manual (friends/relatives are explicitly listed). verification_instrument remains 'unknown' — legitimate finding, no named state form; verification is by activity being 'verifiable' per the ABAWD's own documentation reviewed by the DHR eligibility worker. remote_stance remains 'unknown'; neither the Chapter 7 manual nor the State Plan addresses remote or online volunteer work.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: Chapter 17 POE manual extracted via pypdf confirms '80 hours a month' verbatim — federal_80, monthly_hours_required=80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Chapter 7 policy uses broad public-purpose language with zero org-location or registration requirement; policy is silent on out-of-state restriction; accepts_outofstate_nonprofit='silent'. (3) Geographic waivers: Multiple sources (FY2026 State Plan, Ballotpedia, FNA search results) confirm Alabama has no active ABAWD waivers in 2026 and had none previously; fully enforced statewide. (4) Submission UX: Chapter 17 (newly extracted) specifies four submission channels for the six-month report form: in person (county office), mail, fax, and MyAlabama.gov online portal. ABAWD work-hour changes must be reported by the 10th of the month after the change; ABAWDs receive 4-month certification periods. Reporting cadence is 'other' (neither purely monthly nor at_recertification — it is change-triggered with a 4-month cert cycle). (5) Form required fields: No named state form for volunteer verification found across all passes; defaulted to the standard reasonable baseline.
